# Keyboard Shortcuts

Mastering RiverDrop's keyboard shortcuts will significantly speed up your workflow.

## Navigation

| Shortcut | Action |
| --- | --- |
| **Return** | Open the selected file or folder |
| **Delete** | Move selected local items to Trash or permanently delete remote items |
| **⌘ + 1** | Go to Local Home directory |
| **⌘ + ⇧ + G** | Navigate to a specific Local path |
| **⌘ + R** | Refresh the current directory listing |
| **⌘ + D** | Disconnect from the current remote server |
| **ESC** | Clear current search filter or close panels |

## View & Filters

| Shortcut | Action |
| --- | --- |
| **⌘ + F** | Focus the Search Filter field in the active pane |
| **⌘ + ⇧ + .** | Toggle hidden files in the Local pane |
| **⌘ + ⇧ + ,** | Toggle hidden files in the Remote pane |
| **⌘ + L** | Toggle (Expand/Collapse) the Transfer Log |
| **⌘ + ,** | Open RiverDrop Settings |

## File Operations

| Shortcut | Action |
| --- | --- |
| **⌘ + Click** | Select multiple non-contiguous files |
| **⇧ + Click** | Select a range of files |
| **⌘ + ⇧ + C** | Copy the remote path of the selected files |
| **Space** | (Local) Quick Look for the selected file |

## Customizing Shortcuts
RiverDrop follows standard macOS keyboard conventions. You can customize many of these shortcuts via **System Settings > Keyboard > Keyboard Shortcuts > App Shortcuts**.

::: {.callout-tip}
If you're often searching for files, remember that **⌘+F** followed by **ESC** is the fastest way to find a file and then return to your full project view.
:::
